It all sounds great. I love this I can't afford it Is it high Is it set is it 60 70%? And that's a very different thing from I'm not liquid right now It's kind of not a great time for me that I love that's a qualified lead There's a very big difference from someone saying I'm not really liquid right now Things aren't that great. So it's someone saying I'm broke I'm from Bangladesh You know, I'm from I'm from a part of I'm from New Delhi, India My you know annual income is $2,000. I have no credit card and you're there trying to sell them a $2,000 product Or a $300 product. That's what we call a waste of time and as a salesperson Time is probably your most valuable commodity. How many times a day It's not how many times a day you get off pitches How many times a day do you get a relevant pitches? There's a very big difference to the two How much time are you spending speaking to people that no matter how great you sound? No matter how perfect your solution is They can't buy from you because they can't afford it be honest if that number is Over 30% of the time You've got to change your approach right now You've got to figure out a way to get to that truth Before you enter a full blown sales presentation Because the amount of time you're wasting is it's toxic not only is it toxic But what happens is you start thinking there's something wrong with you. Maybe I'm not closing right Maybe I'm not meant to sales No one can close these people That's the beginning in the end of this story. I don't like them doing because I don't think it's necessary But if they have to they can occasionally go down to 12 payments, right? So if you're selling b2b, you don't really run into research. But if you're selling b2c I'm saying that almost 75 percent of the time the primary objectives. I can't afford it. I and they really don't have the money Don't be scared to ask people about that financial picture now remember one of the core rules of Straight line prospecting Is you don't start off By asking invasive questions It's not going to be the first question. So don't get on the phone and say, okay. It's a young guy So how's your financial picture? Don't know start off with your big picture non-invasive questions first Be smart Very often from those big picture questions You'll find off. You'll you'll see the alarm bells go off You'll say, uh-oh, I might have someone I'm talking to that's not financially qualified And then you make sure you stop positioning your questions and being in more deeply If you Get on the phone someone and you immediately guys. Oh, I owe my own business. You're all clear You got the whole clear sign man. You don't need to ask any questions about financial picture. You don't If you're talking to a business owner for three years, they have the money Or they're gonna lie to you anyway If someone says they own a business, how long you mean? Oh three years. Okay All right next question comes in from victor afratea What are some examples of a low action threshold if you cannot reduce the order size? Lowering the action threshold if you can't reduce the order size There's a lot well lowering if you can't reduce the orders. Well reducing the order size Doesn't lower someone's action threshold Okay, it just um, it changes the energy in benefits out equation In other words when you lower the order size you're asking to make a smaller commitment, right? So their energy expenditure versus what what benefits are they getting? Right, and you're letting them essentially find out if it really is going to be that way Expending less energy money is stored energy, right? Lowering action threshold. Let me explain to you what this is. It's a good question by the way Imagine a scale. Let me draw it out for you the easiest way Guys make sure you watch whiteboard Wednesday on youtube These are all your negatives reasons not to buy These are all your positives reasons to buy right positives negatives As you move someone down the straight line from the open to the close, right? Everything you say everything you do is designed to you knocking out negatives You're building on positives knocking out negatives building on right and eventually the scale tips, right? So when you're running your loops Whenever you loop around you keep adding on positives and you're knocking out negatives, right? Hopefully at the end you get almost all negatives as many enough negatives out So the scale tips in the beginning right that kind of balance But look here the fulcrum is in the middle When you lower someone's action threshold What you're doing is is you are changing the way They process the buying decision their beliefs about buying in other words When you lower someone's action thresholds like pushing the fulcrum over here So the negatives now what happens the negatives have a lot less Power They don't exert as much force when the fulcrum's over here one positive now equals three negatives My father made he rest in peace was this ultra tough Not the crack as a buyer right his beliefs were that my best decisions are made slowly I don't trust people in general Sales people are out to get me People like that That fulcrum is over here People like me i'm over here right to me. Okay, that that that that over here. I'm sorry my dad's foot was over here My fulcrum is over here Where i'm like, yeah, I can trust sales people my best decisions make so for me a negative my fulcrum's here What happens a negative doesn't exert much force When we lower someone's action thresholds what we're doing is we're changing their beliefs about buying And and managing risk and by doing that It causes each negative to not matter as much And causes each positive to matter more One example is when you say to someone, you know, john when you get them very close Say, what's the worst that can possibly happen? Let's say i'm wrong and x y and the product is not as good as I say and you lose 500 dollars We get the cash in your guarantee Is that gonna put is that gonna hurt you really? It's not gonna really affect your life that day. No, will you cash your guarantee? Yeah, we'll part as friends right not going to destroy your life. That will right But on the upside here If i'm even half right And you do even nearly as well as all my other clients in this abuse this product What's gonna happen over the next three months then you start future pacing? So what you're doing is you're showing him that like the risk reward ratio and you're changing his belief And as you do that his focus starts sliding. That's one way another way is through the money back guarantee You get it and then this is something called offsetting language rounds. Those are the ways you really reduce action threshold Changing the size of the order doesn't reduce action threshold. It's energy and benefits out All right, tony clark says hey jordan.
